# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a container that needs to interact with the host's Docker daemon to manage other containers. This is for our container orchestration service. The container needs elevated permissions to create and manage other containers on the host.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM docker:24-dind

RUN apk add --no-cache \
    bash \
    curl \
    jq \
    ca-certificates \
    openssl

RUN addgroup -g 1000 orchestrator && \
    adduser -D -u 1000 -G orchestrator orchestrator

RUN mkdir -p /var/run/docker.sock.d && \
    chown orchestrator:orchestrator /var/run/docker.sock.d

COPY --chown=orchestrator:orchestrator docker-entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh

RUN mkdir -p /app /app/logs /app/config && \
    chown -R orchestrator:orchestrator /app

WORKDIR /app

USER orchestrator

ENV DOCKER_HOST=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
ENV DOCKER_API_VERSION=1.43
ENV ORCHESTRATOR_LOG_LEVEL=info
ENV ORCHESTRATOR_MAX_CONTAINERS=50

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD docker ps > /dev/null 2>&1 || exit 1

VOLUME ["/app/config", "/app/logs"]

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/docker-entrypoint.sh"]
CMD ["tail", "-f", "/dev/null"]
